tool.name: newseq
tool.summary: Create a new sequence record from inline residues and typed metadata

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `newseq` constructs one new sequence record from user-supplied inline residues and shared Epithema metadata fields. The record is validated through the core sequence model and emitted through the standard FASTA output path instead of being assembled as ad hoc text.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface requires an explicit record identifier and inline sequence content. Optional flags allow a free-text description and an explicit molecule kind chosen from `dna`, `rna`, `protein`, or `unknown`.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one validated sequence record through the shared sequence output layer. CLI output is FASTA plus the standard Epithema method summary lines reporting identifier, length, molecule, alphabet, and description state.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema newseq`. Validation currently covers explicit DNA creation, explicit protein creation, whitespace normalization, and invalid residue rejection for declared molecule kinds.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 molecule policy is intentionally conservative. When `--molecule` is omitted, Epithema infers DNA or RNA only from unambiguous nucleotide residue sets and otherwise falls back to `unknown` rather than guessing protein. Identifier omission is not supported in v1.

artifacts.label: Inline DNA creation case fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for the inline DNA creation example used to validate the governed `newseq` surface.

examples.title: Create a DNA record from inline residues
examples.description: Builds one DNA sequence record with an explicit identifier, description, and declared molecule kind.

expected_outputs.label: Created sequence record
expected_outputs.description: A normalized DNA record is emitted as FASTA and reported through the shared method result summary.
